duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ETH fallback in _transferOutAndCallV5 sends funds to aggregationPayload.target (aggregator) instead of aggregationPayload.recipient (user) when swapOutV5 fails
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same logic error where the V5 ETH fallback sends to target instead of recipient, with TECH-H001 providing the clearest V4-vs-V5 comparison and most comprehensive analysis

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ERC20 tokens permanently lost when aggregator swapOutV5 fails in _transferOutAndCallV5 because _dexAggSuccess is ignored and no fallback exists
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing ERC20 fallback in _transferOutAndCallV5 where tokens are transferred to aggregator before the call and never recovered on failure

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: msg.value reused for every ETH-based entry in batchTransferOutAndCallV5 loop, causing double-spend of router ETH or cascading reverts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the classic msg.value-in-loop vulnerability where _transferOutAndCallV5 uses msg.value directly instead of per-item amounts; OPS-H001 rated CRITICAL with clear explanation

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless swapOutV5 on THORChain_Aggregator allows anyone to drain ERC20 tokens held by the aggregator by specifying fromAsset and fromAmount matching the aggregator's balance
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same lack of access control on swapOutV5 allowing token drainage; ECON-H003 provides the most detailed attack economics and proof of signal

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Aggregator swapIn uses address(this).balance instead of swap output delta, attributing entire contract ETH/AVAX balance to the current caller's deposit
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same balance attribution vulnerability in swapIn; TECH-H005 covers both THORChain_Aggregator and AvaxAggregator with the most detailed investigation rationale

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: _transferOutV5 uses transferOutPayload.amount (struct field) instead of msg.value for ETH sends, with no validation that msg.value equals sum of ETH amounts in batchTransferOutV5, enabling ETH drain or permanent lock
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same V5 behavioral change where struct amount replaces msg.value for ETH sends; TECH2-H001 rated CRITICAL and identifies the most severe exploitation (arbitrary ETH drain with msg.value=0)

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: safeApprove in _routerDeposit does not reset allowance to 0 before setting new value, incompatible with USDT-style approve race protection, blocking vault migration/churn
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing approve(0) before approve(amount) pattern in _routerDeposit/safeApprove; TECH-H007 covers both THORChain_Router and AvaxRouter with detailed preconditions. Note: second TECH2-H003 ('Router-to-router migration can brick for USDT-like tokens') is the same root cause

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AvaxAggregator swapIn uses OZ SafeERC20.safeApprove which reverts on non-zero-to-non-zero allowance, causing permanent DoS for fee-on-transfer or USDT-like tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same OZ safeApprove non-zero-to-non-zero revert issue in AvaxAggregator.swapIn; TECH-H008 has more detailed investigation rationale

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AvaxRouter deposit() is public (not private like THORChain_Router._deposit), allowing callers to bypass the expiration check enforced by depositWithExpiry
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same visibility inconsistency where AvaxRouter.deposit() is public vs THORChain_Router._deposit being private; TECH-H009 provides the clearest V4/V5 comparison. Note: second TECH2-H004 ('Public AvaxRouter.deposit()') is the same issue

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: THORChain_Aggregator swapIn approves swapRouter for original amount instead of _safeAmount (balance-diff), leaving stale over-approval for fee-on-transfer tokens that can cause DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same amount/_safeAmount mismatch in swapIn's safeApprove call; ECON2-H014 identifies the most severe consequence (permanent swapIn DoS for USDT-like FoT tokens) combining the over-approval with approve race condition

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless ETH transferOut/transferOutV5 allows anyone to emit TransferOut events with themselves as vault, spoofing outbound transfers to Bifrost observers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same lack of vault authentication for ETH TransferOut event emission; ECON2-H009 has the most detailed attack economics. Note: second TECH2-H005 ('Unprivileged event spoofing') and OPS2-H011 (AvaxRouter variant) cover the same pattern

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ETH_RUNE transferTo uses tx.origin instead of msg.sender, enabling phishing attacks where any intermediate contract can drain user tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same SWC-115 tx.origin vulnerability in transferTo; TECH2-H002 rated CRITICAL with clear description

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unbounded loop in returnVaultAssets over coins array can exceed block gas limit, blocking vault migration during churn
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same unbounded iteration in returnVaultAssets; ECON-H014 provides more detailed attack economics including dust-deposit griefing vector

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee-on-transfer tokens cause outbound accounting discrepancy because transferOut emits nominal amount instead of actual received amount
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same outbound FoT token accounting mismatch where event amount differs from actual transfer; ECON-H016 is the clearest general description covering both routers

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: THORChain_Aggregator rescueFunds uses low-level call without decoding return boolean, causing silent failure for non-standard ERC20s
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same missing return value decoding in rescueFunds; TECH-H017 provides more detailed code analysis

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless swapOut on THORChain_Aggregator can be abused to drain pre-existing ETH held by the aggregator via DEX swap to attacker-controlled recipient
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same risk of swapOut being used to drain accumulated ETH from the aggregator via non-standard swapRouter behavior; OPS2-H012 has more detailed analysis. Note: second TECH2-H007 ('Permissionless swapOut()') is the same issue

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Cross-router migration via transferAllowance/returnVaultAssets approves untrusted external router before calling depositWithExpiry, enabling token theft or stale allowance exploitation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same approve-then-external-call risk in cross-router migration paths; OPS-H013 identifies the more severe hijack scenario and covers THORChain_Router while TECH-H018 covers AvaxRouter with the same pattern
